should_trigger: Our auto-orchestrator has prompts, tools, agent outputs, eval reports, and datasets scattered across folders. Design a resource taxonomy and metadata scheme so agents can find the right artifact.
Build an information architecture for a multi-agent knowledge base. I need categories, facets, lifecycle states, provenance fields, and retrieval paths.
Agents keep using different names for the same task types and handoff documents. Create an organizing system with naming rules, metadata, and relationships.
We need to classify orchestration resources by owner, status, dependency, risk, and use case without forcing everything into one folder hierarchy.
Design the catalog behind an agent router so it can locate tools, decide which resources are current, and trace which outputs were derived from which inputs.
should_not_trigger: Break this project scope into a deliverable-oriented WBS with work packages and acceptance criteria.
Sequence these tightly coupled engineering tasks and find which ones can run in parallel using a dependency matrix.
Summarize The Discipline of Organizing in three sentences for a reading list.
edge_cases: We only have six files today, but this will become a reusable agent memory store. Decide whether a lightweight naming convention is enough or whether we need a fuller organizing system.
The resource categories are not stable yet because discovery is still ongoing. Propose a provisional organization that can evolve without breaking retrieval.
